codeReview.summary: The feature successfully replaces the plain dashboard loading text with reusable animated skeletons across the targeted routes, and the interviews/specs pages keep their headers visible while data is loading. I found two non-blocking gaps: the dashboard home skeleton does not closely match the eventual quick-actions layout, and the added tests only exercise the helper components rather than the actual route loading branches.

issues.description: The loading state renders `SkeletonActivityItem` rows in both dashboard section cards, but the loaded page's left-hand section is a `quick-actions-grid` of large action cards. This still meets the 'no plain loading text' requirement, but it does not approximate the final dashboard layout particularly well and will cause a more noticeable layout jump when the real quick actions appear.

The new 'dashboard loading states' tests render skeleton helper components in isolation instead of mounting the actual route components, so they do not verify the real loading branches for `/dashboard`, `/dashboard/projects/$projectId`, `/dashboard/interviews`, or `/dashboard/specs`. Route-wiring regressions such as missing headers or accidentally reintroducing loading text in a page branch would not be caught by this test file.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The web-worker skill's documented procedure does not match what this worker session actually did, but the handoff still marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` as true. The skill says to write tests first and to do manual agent-browser verification, while the transcript shows implementation edits before the test file was created and no agent-browser usage at all.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/Users/ashishhuddar/scopepm/.factory/skills/web-worker/SKILL.md:24` says 'Write tests first (TDD)' and `:41-45` require manual verification with agent-browser. The transcript skeleton for worker session `873daa06-c19c-40d7-9595-30a929666f04` shows `Skeleton.tsx` and route edits occurring before `packages/web/app/__tests__/routes/loading-skeletons.test.tsx` was created, and it contains no agent-browser tool invocation. The handoff file `/Users/ashishhuddar/.factory/missions/8d555b3e-2948-4f0c-abd1-2fe8dc0952a9/handoffs/2026-03-14T22-18-01-683Z__loading-skeletons__873daa06-c19c-40d7-9595-30a929666f04.json:105` still records `followedProcedure: true`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission guidance around removing `loading-text` is broader than the actual feature requirement and pushed unrelated UI churn. The worker had to replace empty-state text styling, not just loading placeholders, because the validation guidance treated any `loading-text` usage as a failure even though the shared CSS class is also a generic centered text style.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The validation contract for `loading-skeletons` requires `grep -r 'loading-text' packages/web/app/routes/ returns nothing`. The transcript shows follow-up edits made only after that grep failed. In the final code, unrelated empty-state paragraphs were rewritten inline at `packages/web/app/routes/dashboard/index.tsx:219` and `packages/web/app/routes/dashboard/projects.$projectId.tsx:164` and `:190`, while the shared style itself still exists at `packages/web/app/styles/landing.css:1588`.

summary: Reviewed the feature metadata, validation contract, AGENTS guidance, worker handoff, transcript skeleton, commit b6be5ce diff, and the resulting web route/component/test files. Result: pass with two non-blocking findings — the dashboard home skeleton could better match the eventual quick-actions layout, and the added tests do not mount the real route loading branches; I also noted skill/procedure and validation-guidance shared-state gaps.
